behavior_summary: U8P_ALTAR_EW::look: When the player examines the altar it shows a short description string "Altar of Focus" (brief U8P_ALTAR_EW::look handler).

U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit: When the altar is struck or used with an item (incoming selector param), the script checks the item's type and frame. If the item matches expected criteria it plays sound effects, triggers altar NPC animations, creates and pops a temporary/spawned item into the world, updates item frames/counters, sets a global state indicating the altar now owns a focus (hasOwnFoci = 1), and delegates additional placement/progression logic to U8P_ALTAR_EW::func0270.

U8P_ALTAR_EW::func0270: A helper that creates/pops a different spawn item at coordinates derived from the provided reference, increments/maintains a local counter/frame for progression, chooses between two SFX variants on a threshold via a small random branch, sets the spawned item's visual frame, may spawn a short-lived helper process for timing, and destroys the spawned item if counters exceed an upper threshold.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit ignores the call immediately if param1 == 0 (no selector).
Item type check: U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit requires the incoming item to be type 0x018C and uses its frame to gate behavior (accepted when frame < 0x08, or when frame == 0x10).

On a successful match U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit: several SFX calls bracket the visual work, Npc::getDir is read to orient animations, multiple Npc::doAnim processes are spawned and the code suspends/waits on them.

U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit creates an item (type observed as 0x00CA) and pops it at coordinates derived from the incoming item (Z+5, Y+offset, X+offset). It initializes a local frame/counter (local4 = 0), sets the spawned item's frame and increments local4, and treats a special-case when local4 == 0x05 to update the original item's frame (+0x08 or set to 0x11). If local4 > 0x0D the spawned item is destroyed and a concluding animation is played.

U8P_ALTAR_EW::func0270 creates an item (type observed as 0x01E1) at Z+5,Y,X of the provided reference, increments a local counter (local3) each run, and uses thresholds (local3 > 0x18 and > 0x23) to either reset/trigger SFX (with a small urandom choice) or destroy the spawned item when counters are too high; otherwise it sets the spawned item's frame and spawns a short helper before suspending.
open_questions: Exact gameplay meaning of the checked item types/frames (e.g., type 0x018C and frames like <0x08, ==0x05, ==0x10) — are these specific focus items, charges, or upgrade states? (inferred, medium confidence)

Whether the local counters/frames (local3/local4) persist across multiple hits or are ephemeral per process invocation — metadata shows them as locals so persistence across separate hits is unclear. (low confidence)
Are the spawned items collectible/usable focus objects for the player, or purely visual effects? (inferred from create/pop calls but not explicit, low confidence)
Precise player-visible result and how hasOwnFoci integrates with other game systems (e.g., is it used as a permanence marker or a gating flag)? (low confidence)

main_function.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examination description for the altar (player-facing 'U8P_ALTAR_EW::look' text).
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player examines or 'looks at' the altar object/NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::gotHit
function_map.role: interaction handler for when the altar is struck/used with an item; validates item, plays SFX/animations, spawns/pop items, updates frames and sets hasOwnFoci

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR_EW::func0270
function_map.role: spawn/placement and progression helper for focus-related objects; manages a local counter and SFX/cleanup logic

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate spawn/placement and progression logic for the focus-related object created during the hit/interaction sequence.

external_calls.purpose: Query the incoming item's type to decide whether the altar should respond.
Read the incoming item's frame/state to branch behavior (checks for <0x08, ==0x05, ==0x10 and updates frames later).
Read altar/NPC facing to orient animations triggered during the interaction.
Start altar/NPC animations as visual feedback for the hit/interaction; processes are spawned and the code suspends/waits on them.
Create a temporary/spawned item (observed type 0x00CA) as part of the altar's response.
Place the created item into the world at coordinates computed from the incoming item's position (Z+5, Y+X offsets).
Set the spawned item's visual frame to reflect progression and to update the original item's frame in a special case.
Destroy the spawned item when a local progression counter exceeds a threshold.
Play sound effects before/after animations and spawning to provide audio feedback.
Spawn a short-lived helper/process for timing or visual-effect delay during the interaction.
Create the helper/spawned item (observed type 0x01E1) used by the placement/progression logic.
Place the created helper/spawned item into the world using coordinates derived from the input reference (Z+5, Y, X).
Read coordinates from the provided reference to compute pop placement.
Read the reference's frame to influence branching and SFX decisions.
Set the spawned item's visual frame to reflect the local counter (progression).
Destroy the spawned item when the helper's local counter exceeds an upper threshold.
Play one of two SFX variants when a lower threshold is crossed (selected via urandom).
Choose between SFX variants with a small random chance.
Spawn a short helper/process after setting the spawned item's frame, likely for timing/delay, then suspend.

writes.name: hasOwnFoci
writes.likely_effect: Marked (set to 1) when the altar yields or accepts a focus; serves as a state indicator that the altar has produced/owns a focus.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ALTAR_EW::look: When the player examines the altar it shows a short description string "Altar of Focus" (brief U8P_ALTAR_EW::look handler).
